content:
First, observe that for any choice of the three “data‐points”  
\[p(5)=A,\quad p(15)=M,\quad p(25)=B,\]  
one can write the unique cubic through them as  
\[r(x)=A\frac{(x-15)(x-25)}{(5-15)(5-25)}\;+\;M\frac{(x-5)(x-25)}{(15-5)(15-25)}\;+\;B\frac{(x-5)(x-15)}{(25-5)(25-15)}\,,\]  
and then add any integer multiple of  
\[(x-5)(x-15)(x-25)=x^3-45x^2+575x-1875\]  
to make all four coefficients integers.  Thus the only integrality constraint on \((A,M,B)\) comes from forcing the quadratic part of \(r(x)\) to have integer coefficients.

Since we are given  
\[
A+B = p(5)+p(25)=1906,
\] 
set \(B=1906-A\).  A routine check (clearing denominators 200,100,200 in the Lagrange formula) shows that the necessary and sufficient congruences reduce to
\[
M\equiv 53\pmod{100}.
\]
Hence 
\[
p(15)=M=100t+53\quad(t\in\Bbb Z),
\]
and the integer closest to zero of that form is \(M=-47\) (taking \(t=-1\)).  Thus the minimum possible value of 
\(|p(15)|\)
is
\[
\boxed{47}.
\]

message.content:
First, observe that for any choice of the three “data‐points”  
\[p(5)=A,\quad p(15)=M,\quad p(25)=B,\]  
one can write the unique cubic through them as  
\[r(x)=A\frac{(x-15)(x-25)}{(5-15)(5-25)}\;+\;M\frac{(x-5)(x-25)}{(15-5)(15-25)}\;+\;B\frac{(x-5)(x-15)}{(25-5)(25-15)}\,,\]  
and then add any integer multiple of  
\[(x-5)(x-15)(x-25)=x^3-45x^2+575x-1875\]  
to make all four coefficients integers.  Thus the only integrality constraint on \((A,M,B)\) comes from forcing the quadratic part of \(r(x)\) to have integer coefficients.

Since we are given  
\[
A+B = p(5)+p(25)=1906,
\] 
set \(B=1906-A\).  A routine check (clearing denominators 200,100,200 in the Lagrange formula) shows that the necessary and sufficient congruences reduce to
\[
M\equiv 53\pmod{100}.
\]
Hence 
\[
p(15)=M=100t+53\quad(t\in\Bbb Z),
\]
and the integer closest to zero of that form is \(M=-47\) (taking \(t=-1\)).  Thus the minimum possible value of 
\(|p(15)|\)
is
\[
\boxed{47}.
\]
